data.title: Bitcoin sees bank participation curbed by 1,250% Basel rule
data.description:
Basel crypto capital rules set a 1,250% risk weight that keeps banks' Bitcoin in Group 2, while U. S. regulators now review calibration and exposure caps.
source: https://coincu.com/news/bitcoin-sees-bank-participation-curbed-by-1250-basel-rule/

data.title: India's Crude Imports from Saudi Arabia Reach Six-Year High Amid U.S. Pressure
data.description: India is poised to import the highest volume of crude oil from Saudi Arabia in over six years this month. Bloomberg posted on X that this increase comes as the South Asian country encounters ongoing pressure from the United States to decrease its acquisition of Russian oil. The shift in India's import strategy highlights the geopolitical dynamics influencing global energy markets. As India navigates these pressures, its reliance on Saudi crude underscores the complex interplay between energy needs and international relations.

data.title: Central Bank Leadership Changes Raise Concerns in Europe
data.description: Recent changes in the leadership of Europe's central banks have sparked concerns about the region's financial stability. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the potential implications of these shifts in power. The reshuffle involves several key figures in the European financial landscape, raising questions about the future direction of monetary policy. Observers are closely monitoring these developments, as they could have significant impacts on the European economy. The changes come at a critical time, with Europe facing various economic challenges that require stable and effective leadership in its central banking institutions.

data.title: Hyperliquid Policy Center CEO: Opposes forced surveillance of unmanaged developers; financial privacy is a fundamental right.
data.description: Jake Chervinsky, CEO of the Hyperliquid Policy Center, a lobbying group for Hyperliquid, stated in an article on the X platform that front-end interfaces connecting to decentralized trading protocols should not be required to perform KYC. He argued that US law does not, and should not, force non-custodial software developers to "monitor" users without a warrant, as financial privacy is a fundamental right. While regulators cannot turn a blind eye to illicit on-chain financial activities, even those far smaller than those in the traditional financial system (TradFi), the solution is not simply to apply old regulations that exclude billions of people from the financial system to new technologies that can provide better solutions.

data.title: Hyperliquid CEO Advocates Against KYC for Decentralized Protocol Interfaces
data.description: Jake Chervinsky, CEO of Hyperliquid Policy Center, expressed on the X platform that front-end interfaces connecting decentralized trading protocols should not be required to implement Know Your Customer (KYC) procedures. According to Odaily, Chervinsky argued that U.S. law does not and should not compel non-custodial software developers to monitor users without a warrant, emphasizing that financial privacy is a fundamental right. While regulatory bodies cannot ignore illegal financial activities on-chain, even if they are smaller in scale compared to the traditional financial system (TradFi), the solution is not to apply outdated regulatory rules that exclude billions from the financial system to new technologies that offer better solutions.

data.title: India's Economic Activity Gains Momentum in February
data.description: India's economic activity saw an uptick in February, driven by improved sentiment following the reduction of tariffs on Indian goods by the United States. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the findings from a flash survey conducted by HSBC. The survey indicated that the easing of trade barriers contributed to a more optimistic outlook among businesses and consumers, boosting economic performance during the month. This development is seen as a positive sign for India's economy, which has been navigating various challenges in recent times.

data.title: Planet Midday News
data.description:
1. Dragonfly Partner: Smart contracts cannot replace legal contracts; cryptocurrencies are more like machines than humans.
2. Robinhood: January event contract trading volume reached 3.4 billion contracts, and cryptocurrency trading volume reached $22.9 billion.
3. Polymarket: The probability of the US government confirming the existence of aliens by 2027 has surged to 24%.
4. SlowMist Cosine: Malicious skills discovered on ClawHub market, potentially stealing SSH keys, encrypted wallets, etc.
5. Three whales shorting ETH have accumulated over $24 million in unrealized profits.
6. A newly created address withdrew 7,000 ETH from Binance in the past hour, worth $13.55 million.
7. Upbit and Bithumb will list Aztek (AZTEC) Korean Won trading pairs.
8. Bloomberg Analyst: Bitcoin ETFs have seen net inflows of $53 billion over two years; Wall Street's influence continues to be positive.
9. A whale holding 105,000 ETH long positions has accumulated over $8.522 million in unrealized losses.
10. Strategy CEO: If the US wants to become the "global crypto capital," it should reassess the Basel Capital Rules.

data.title: Hungary's Mol Faces Challenges with Russian Crude Dependency
data.description:
Hungary's energy company Mol is experiencing difficulties due to its reliance on Russian crude oil. Bloomberg posted on X that the company's strategy, which heavily depends on Russian oil imports, is now facing significant challenges. This dependency has become a critical issue as geopolitical tensions and market dynamics shift.

Mol's situation highlights the broader implications of energy reliance on specific sources, particularly in the context of fluctuating international relations. The company's future strategies may need to adapt to these changing circumstances to ensure stability and growth.

As the global energy landscape evolves, Mol's experience serves as a reminder of the complexities involved in energy sourcing and the potential risks associated with over-reliance on a single supplier.

data.title: U.S. Urged to Reevaluate Basel Framework for Digital Assets
data.description: Strategy CEO Phong Le recently shared insights on the X platform regarding the impact of the Basel Accord on global banking standards and asset risk-weighting rules. According to Odaily, these frameworks significantly influence how banks engage in digital asset-related activities, including Bitcoin. The Basel Committee, comprising central banks and regulatory bodies from 28 jurisdictions, is responsible for these rules, with the United States being one of the participating countries. Le emphasized that if the U.S. aims to become the 'global crypto capital,' it is crucial to reassess the implementation of capital requirements and risk weights under the Basel framework.

data.title: Malaysia's Export Growth Accelerates in January Amid Electronics Surge
data.description: Malaysia's exports experienced significant growth in January, marking the fastest pace in over three years. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the surge in electronics shipments as a key driver of this increase. The rise in exports reflects strong demand in the global market, particularly for electronic products, which have become a major component of Malaysia's trade portfolio. This growth comes amid broader economic trends and signals positive momentum for the country's trade sector. Analysts suggest that the electronics industry will continue to play a crucial role in Malaysia's export performance in the coming months.

data.title: Europe Strengthens Security Ahead of French Presidential Election
data.description: Europe is intensifying efforts to protect its key institutions as France approaches its presidential election. Bloomberg posted on X that recent surveys indicate a significant lead for the far-right National Rally party. This development has prompted European nations to bolster security measures to ensure the stability and integrity of their political and economic systems. The heightened precautions reflect concerns over potential disruptions that could arise from the election outcome. Authorities are focused on maintaining order and safeguarding democratic processes during this critical period.

data.title: Bitcoin's Shutdown Price: Understanding Its Role in Mining Operations
data.description: The concept of Bitcoin's 'shutdown price' is often discussed in the context of mining operations. According to NS3.AI, this price level is perceived as the threshold below which miners may cease their activities. The article examines whether this shutdown price truly represents the bottom line for miners or merely serves as a market filter amid volatile conditions. It seeks to address common misconceptions surrounding this term in relation to Bitcoin mining and market dynamics.

data.title: Uniswap Labs Launches Beta Developer Platform for Swift Integration
data.description: Uniswap Labs has introduced the beta version of its Developer Platform, designed to streamline the integration of Uniswap's swapping and liquidity functions through API keys. According to NS3.AI, this platform allows developers to incorporate these functionalities into their applications swiftly, with integration possible within minutes. Furthermore, the platform includes a new Skill feature that supports proxy-based workflows, offering one-click API access via command line tools.

data.title: Ethical Concerns Impact Progress of CLARITY Act in Crypto Legislation
data.description: Ethical concerns surrounding the Trump family's involvement in cryptocurrency are affecting the advancement of the CLARITY Act, a crucial piece of legislation aimed at structuring the crypto market. According to NS3.AI, Caitlin Long has pointed out that opposition, particularly from Senator Elizabeth Warren, poses a threat to the bipartisan support essential for the bill's passage. Despite these challenges, Senators Cynthia Lummis and Kirsten Gillibrand are continuing their bipartisan efforts to advance crypto regulatory reform.

data.title: Bithumb will list Aztek (AZTEC) Korean Won trading pair
data.description: According to an official announcement, Bithumb will launch a trading pair for Aztek (AZTEC) Korean Won.

data.title: Bloomberg analysts: Bitcoin ETFs have seen net inflows of $53 billion over two years, with Wall Street influence continuing to be positive.
data.description: Eric Balchunas, a senior ETF analyst at Bloomberg, pointed out in an article on the X platform that the cumulative net inflow of Bitcoin ETFs (the most important data) peaked at $63 billion last October and is currently at $53 billion, meaning a net increase of $53 billion in just two years. He stated that his team (more optimistic than most of their peers) originally predicted inflows of only $5-15 billion in the first year. This data is of great reference value for understanding the $8 billion ETF outflow after Bitcoin's 45% drop and the relationship between Bitcoin and Wall Street. Currently, the overall influence of Wall Street continues to be positive.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $130 million yesterday, with BlackRock's ETHA experiencing the largest net outflow at $96.7977 million.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $130 million yesterday (February 19th, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was BlackRock ETF ETHA, with a net outflow of $96.7977 million. ETHA's historical total net inflow has reached $11.873 billion. This was followed by Grayscale Ethereum Mini Trust ETF ETH, with a single-day net outflow of $18.4365 million. ETH's historical total net inflow has reached $1.702 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs is $11.004 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) of 4.67%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $11.525 billion.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $166 million yesterday, marking the third consecutive day of net outflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $166 million yesterday (February 19th, Eastern Time). The BlackRock ETF (IBIT) experienced the largest single-day net outflow, at $164 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $61.238 billion. The Valkyrie ETF (BRRR) followed with a net outflow of $1.7013 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $314 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $84.37 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of total Bitcoin market capitalization) of 6.28%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $53.925 billion.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Former South Korean President Yoon Seok-yeol Responds to Martial Law Decision and Life Sentence
data.description: Former South Korean President Yoon Seok-yeol has expressed his stance on the declaration of martial law, stating it was a 'salvation decision' made for the nation and its citizens. According to Jin10, Yoon conveyed his sentiments through his legal team, acknowledging the difficulties his actions may have caused due to his 'own shortcomings.' Additionally, Yoon has publicly rejected the life sentence handed down in his first trial, indicating his inability to accept the verdict.
